title: Tamper evident package
abstract: 1. A combination (LC1 ) of a package (200) and a sealing label (100), wherein the package (200) comprises varnished cardboard (230), wherein the package (200) comprises a first rupture region (REG1) formed of the varnished cardboard (230), wherein the first rupture region (REG1) comprises one or more structural deviations (DEV1) such that a tear strength (S1) of the first rupture region (REG1) is in the range of 2% to 50% of an average tear strength (SAVE) of the varnished cardboard (230) of the package (200), and wherein the sealing label (100) has been attached to the first rupture region (REG1).
claims:
Claims 1. A combination (LC1 ) of a package (200) and a sealing label (100), wherein the package (200) comprises varnished cardboard (230), wherein the package (200) comprises a first rupture region (REG1 ) formed of the varnished cardboard (230), wherein the first rupture region (REG1 ) comprises one or more structural deviations (DEV1 ) such that a tear strength (S1 ) of the first rupture region (REG1 ) is in the range of 2% to 50% of an average tear strength (SAVE) of the varnished cardboard (230) of the package (200), and wherein the sealing label (100) has been attached to the first rupture region (REG1 ). 2. The combination (LC1 ) of claim 1 , wherein a minimum detaching force (FA) of the label (100) is greater than a first breaking force (FBI ), wherein the first breaking force (F BI ) is first pulling force which causes breaking of the first rupture region (REG1 ) in a situation where the label (100) is separated from the varnished cardboard (230) by pulling the label (100) with said pulling first force, and wherein the minimum detaching force (F A ) is a second pulling force which is needed to separate the adhesive layer (20) of the label (100) from the surface of the varnished cardboard (230) in a situation where the label (100) is pulled with said second pulling force. 3. The combination (LC1) of claim 1 or 2, wherein the package (200) comprises a second rupture region (REG2), the package (200) comprises an opening joint (201 ) between the first rupture region (REG1 ) and the second rupture region (REG2), wherein the sealing label (100) overlaps the first rupture region (REG1 ), the second rupture region (REG2), and the opening joint (201 ). 4. The combination (LC1 ) according to any of the claims 1 to 3 wherein the varnish layer (232) of the package (200) covers the first rupture region (REG1 ). 5. The combination (LC1 ) according to any of the claims 1 to 4 wherein the one or more structural deviations (DEV1 ) of the first rupture region (REG1 ) 

 are located on the inner side of the package (200) such that they are not visible from the outside of the package (200). 6. The combination (LC1 ) according to any of the claims 1 to 5 wherein at least one of the structural deviations (DEV1 ) is an incision, a groove, a hole, a perforation, or a thin portion whose thickness is smaller than an average thickness of the varnished cardboard. 7. The combination (LC1 ) according to any of the claims 1 to 6 wherein at least one of the structural deviations (DEV1 ) has been formed by thermally degrading a localized region of the cardboard of the package, chemically degrading a localized region of the cardboard, by processing the cardboard with a laser beam and/or by mechanically removing material from the cardboard. 8. The combination (LC1 ) according to any of the claims 1 to 7 wherein the structural deviations (DEV1 ) of the first rupture region (REG1 ) do not extend to the outer surface of the package (200). 9. The combination (LC1 ) according to any of the claims 1 to 8 wherein the width (WREGI ) of the first rupture region (REG1 ) in the direction of the opening joint (201 ) is smaller than or equal to the width (wioo) of the label (100). 10. The combination (LC1 ) according to any of the claims 1 to 9 wherein the package (200) comprises a marking (MRK1 ), the label (200) is substantially transparent, the label (200) at least partly overlaps the marking (MRK1 ) such that the marking (MRK1 ) is visually detectable through the label (200). 11. The combination (LC1 ) according to any of the claims 1 to 10, wherein the lower side of the rupture region (REG1 ) is supported by an auxiliary layer of cardboard. 12. The combination (LC1 ) according to any of the claims 1 to 11 , wherein the package (200) comprises a pharmaceutical product (PROD1 ). 13. A package (200) comprising varnished cardboard (230), wherein the package (200) comprises a first rupture region (REG1 ) formed of the varnished cardboard (230), wherein the first rupture region (REG1 ) comprises one or more structural deviations (DEV1 ) such that a tear strength (S1 ) of the first rupture region (REG1 ) is in the range of 2% to 50% of an average tear strength (SAVE) of the varnished cardboard (230) of the package (200).
